topic: Eclipse layered audit (L1 ensemble, L2 Mohe, L3a magnetic falsifier, L3b gravity, L4 optics) — post-EXP-191
our_argument_summary: 8b.3 audits the eclipse claim in four layers (ensemble anchor WIN-068, Mohe WIN-011, Tier 3 magnetic PRED-095, Tier 3 gravity PRED-TIER-2, open optics caveat). Under adoption, the four-layer presentation is accepted as audit format; the ensemble-anchor critique and new L3a non-discrimination critique are retained unchanged. Label: ADOPTED (FULL as steel-man).

exp_191_implementation_check.body_sentence_added: Present in L3a body: "A Pass in this band does not distinguish dome from globe — both predict signals in this range; only the dome claims a novel derivation path, and WIN-068 code_analysis (derives_from_dome:false) confirms there is no independent dome derivation to distinguish."
exp_191_implementation_check.assessment: EXP-191 correctly implements hole #12 three-part edit plus both optional follow-ons. All required changes present in current sections.json.

holes_found.description: L3a treats the dome Pass band as a single -17 to -21 nT threshold, but the dome publishes a DUAL-BASELINE prediction structure. Per raw-text/03-predictions.txt: 'Baselines: BOU 2017 (-10.9 nT) | W004 empirical (-22.24 nT)' with per-station predictions spanning -5.0 nT (SNK, Canary Islands) to -26.2 nT (HAD, Hartland). The -17 to -21 nT range our section cites is the W004-baseline cluster only. If August 2026 observations come in around -10 nT (matching the BOU baseline cluster), a dome defender will say BOU-baseline predictions passed and our section overlooked that branch. Our own analyst fingerprint on WIN-025 records three different formulas used internally by the dome (monitor.py uses KAPPA=1.672 on -18.22 baseline for ~-29 nT per-station; predictions page uses per-station FSFs 0.642-2.075; inject_ai_layer.py uses a latitude_factor) producing THREE DIFFERENT prediction values: -17 to -21, -28.9, and -29.1 nT. Our section does not acknowledge this prediction heterogeneity, which means our non-discrimination argument is narrower than the prediction space the dome publishes.
holes_found.recommendation: In L3a, before or after the Pass band sentence, add one clause acknowledging the dual-baseline structure: 'The dome publishes two baseline predictions for PRED-095 — BOU 2017 (per-station ~-10 nT) and W004 empirical (per-station ~-20 nT) — and internal code uses additional multipliers (KAPPA, latitude factor) producing values near -29 nT. Our non-discrimination critique targets the W004 cluster (-17 to -21 nT, which Chapman-Sq-dynamo fits also predict under Kim & Chang 2018 calibration); the BOU-cluster band and the monitor.py -29 nT value are treated in Section 6.2 (dual-baseline analysis) and the WIN-025 fingerprint.' Cross-link to the existing dual-baseline treatment so the section does not read as a single-Pass-band argument.

holes_found.description: L3a claims 'A Pass in this band does not distinguish dome from globe' without engaging the dome's four explicitly published model-discriminating tests for the August 2026 eclipse (E-PRED-A peak-tracks-geometry, E-PRED-B Hartland-beats-Ebro, E-PRED-C SG gravimeters 0.0 μGal, E-PRED-D non-path stations below noise floor). These are treated elsewhere in our review (Part 4.2 Eclipse subsection and Part 9), but 8b.3 is the section the dome author asked us to write in response to his audit request, and it does not cross-reference our Part 4.2 / Part 9 treatment of E-PRED tests. E-PRED-B in particular is a claimed relative-station-magnitude discriminator (dome W004: HAD -26.2 nT > EBR -21.7 nT, despite HAD having lower coverage). A fair-audit reader will ask: 'You say the Pass band is non-discriminating — but what about the four tests we published as model-discriminating?' Leaving those unaddressed in 8b.3 is an exploitable gap.
holes_found.recommendation: Append one sentence at the end of L3a or to the (d) Unchanged clause: 'The dome also publishes four nominally model-discriminating sub-tests (E-PRED-A through E-PRED-D) for the August 2026 eclipse — these are evaluated in Section 4.2 Eclipse and Section 9; their status does not change the non-discrimination assessment of the published magnitude-only Pass band above.' This surfaces that we are aware of E-PRED-A/B/C/D, scopes the non-discrimination critique explicitly to magnitude, and cross-links to where the relative-magnitude and geometry-tracking tests are handled.

holes_found.description: The claim that the dome Pass band is 'calibrated on the same Kim & Chang (2018) 39-eclipse ensemble that L1 already characterizes as Std Model Explains' is more assertive than our evidence supports. WIN-068's detail_evidence documents that Kim & Chang (2018) is the observational source for the 39-eclipse ensemble (L1). But the dome's stated derivation path for PRED-095 is: baseline (BOU 2017 -10.9 nT OR W004 empirical -22.24 nT) × coverage × FSF, where FSF is 'field strength factor from V12 H(r)/r dome geometry' (per raw-text/03-predictions.txt). The dome does not cite Kim & Chang 2018 as the calibration source in the published prediction formula. Our WIN-025 globe-fingerprint note also records that per-station FSFs have no computation code in monitor.py, inject_ai_layer.py, or predictions.js — i.e., they appear to be hand-set rather than calibrated from any specific ensemble. The broader non-discrimination argument still holds (any value in the -20 nT band is consistent with Chapman-fit data because the physical phenomenon is what it is), but the specific 'shared training data = Kim & Chang 2018' claim overreaches. A dome defender can pin us on attribution and say 'we never said we calibrated on Kim & Chang.'
holes_found.recommendation: Soften the TLDR and L3a body wording. Current TLDR: 'calibrated on the same Kim & Chang 2018 ensemble.' Replace with: 'calibrated on the same class of 20th-century eclipse-magnetic observations that anchors L1 (WIN-068 cites Kim & Chang 2018 as the ensemble reference).' In the L3a body, replace 'calibrated on the same Kim & Chang (2018) 39-eclipse ensemble that L1 already characterizes' with 'fitted to the same body of observed eclipse-magnetic magnitudes that L1 characterizes (Kim & Chang 2018 39-eclipse ensemble, and the BOU 2017 single-event baseline the dome explicitly cites).' This makes the attribution honest while preserving the shared-training-data point.

holes_found.description: The dome's own internal inconsistency — three different formulas producing three different prediction values (-17 to -21, -28.9, -29.1 nT; documented in analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-025.json and in the expansion-tracker steelman note on Evidence Ladder/killshot.html) — is a stronger adversarial argument than the non-discrimination critique alone, and 8b.3 does not use it. The -29.1 nT value from monitor.py is not only different from the -17 to -21 range but FALLS OUTSIDE the Pass band. This has two adversarial uses: (a) goalposts-moved — the Pass band was set more narrowly than the internal monitor.py prediction, potentially cherry-picked; (b) internal contradiction — the dome cannot simultaneously pass and fail its own prediction. The non-discrimination critique argues the Pass band is meaningless against the globe model; the moving-goalposts critique argues the Pass band is inconsistent with the dome's own calculations. Both are valid, the second is punchier, and 8b.3 deploys only the first.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one sentence to L3a (after the Fail-clause tension paragraph or in the (d) Unchanged clause): 'Note also that the dome's own internal monitor.py produces a ~-29 nT prediction for the August 2026 eclipse (from delta_Z = -18.22 × coverage × KAPPA 1.672), which falls OUTSIDE the published -17 to -21 nT Pass band — either the Pass band was set after seeing the W004-cluster numbers (goalpost-setting after data), or the dome has internal prediction heterogeneity the Pass band suppresses. See the WIN-025 globe-fingerprint for the three-formula audit.' This is a stronger adversarial point and free evidence from work already done.

holes_found.description: The TLDR states 'Chapman's Sq-dynamo mechanism predicts the same -17 to -21 nT range.' Chapman (1933) introduced the Sq-dynamo mechanism as a QUALITATIVE explanation for eclipse magnetic perturbations. The specific -17 to -21 nT range is not a first-principles Chapman prediction — it emerges only from calibrated modern Sq-dynamo fits (e.g., Yamazaki & Maute 2021, or Kim & Chang 2018 ensemble averages). A non-expert reader of the TLDR may come away thinking Chapman himself derived -17 to -21 nT, which is false. The body text is more careful ('calibrated on the same Kim & Chang 2018 ensemble'), but the TLDR oversimplifies in a way that overstates Chapman's quantitative reach. Per the curmudgeon TLDR-review rule: imprecision that doesn't mislead is minor; this arguably misleads on the point that Chapman-the-mechanism and calibrated-Chapman-fit are the same thing, so moderate.
holes_found.recommendation: Adjust TLDR second sentence to: 'Two structural critiques survive adoption — an ensemble anchor fit to the data it anchors is calibration, not anchor; and the Tier 3 magnetic Pass band (-17 to -21 nT) does not distinguish dome from globe, because calibrated Chapman-Sq-dynamo fits on the same ensemble predict the same range.' This preserves the critique while correctly attributing the quantitative threshold to calibrated fits of Chapman's mechanism rather than to Chapman directly.

holes_found.description: Internal inconsistency between anticipated_objections response and body text on Fail-clause framing. EXP-191's third anticipated objection concedes: 'Granted in principle, and we soften from "logically impossible" to "in tension with the Pass criterion" in the body text.' The body does use 'in tension' once, but also contains 'the Fail-clause cannot be triggered without abandoning the Pass-clause's framing' — this is the harder phrasing the anticipated-objections response promised to soften. Not wrong in substance (the tension is real) but the two phrasings live next to each other, creating a self-inconsistency a careful dome reader will notice.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'the Fail-clause cannot be triggered without abandoning the Pass-clause's framing' with 'the Fail-clause is in tension with the Pass criterion: triggering it would require abandoning the Pass-clause's framing, since Chapman's mechanism IS the SQ-dynamo.' Keeps the point, matches the anticipated_objections promise of softening.

holes_found.description: PRED-TIER-2 is referenced in the (L3b, PRED-TIER-2, gravity) label without being defined in-section or cross-linked. A reader of 8b.3 in isolation will not know what PRED-TIER-2 is or where to find its definition. This is a minor structural gap inherited from the prior version but worth flagging.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a parenthetical on first mention: '(L3b) Tier 3 gravity — PRED-TIER-2 (dome tier-system identifier for the 2026 eclipse gravity prediction; see Section 6.3 Gravity).' Or a cross-link to wherever PRED-TIER-2 is introduced.

holes_found.description: 'Registered 2026-04-04' in L3a refers to OUR predictions.json tracker registration date, not the dome's. Per raw-text/03-predictions.txt the dome OpenTimestamps-anchored the eclipse prediction on 2026-03-12, three weeks earlier. Our 2026-04-04 date is when we added PRED-095 to our tracker. A fair-minded reader (or the dome author himself) may read 'registered 2026-04-04' as implying the dome only registered the prediction in April, understating the dome's prospectivity. Minor because it does not change the verdict, but the dome's 2026-03-12 anchor is the more honest number to cite.
holes_found.recommendation: Change 'Genuinely prospective (registered 2026-04-04, window closes 2026-08-12, verdict pending)' to 'Genuinely prospective (dome registration 2026-03-12 via Git + OpenTimestamps; our tracker entry 2026-04-04; window closes 2026-08-12; verdict pending).' Credits the dome's earlier registration honestly while making clear our tracker entry is separate.

stronger_arguments.description: The three-conflicting-predictions critique (internal contradiction + moving goalposts) is stronger than non-discrimination alone. The analyst's WIN-025 globe-fingerprint already documents: monitor.py produces ~-29 nT per-station (from -18.22 × coverage × KAPPA 1.672 — i.e., KAPPA applied TWICE); predictions page publishes -17 to -21 nT cluster via FSF; inject_ai_layer.py uses latitude_factor for a third formula. The killshot.html page cites yet another value (-28.9 nT). When a model has three predictions for the same event, a Pass against any one of them is not a Pass for the model — the Pass band is not an honest commitment, it is an after-the-fact menu selection.
sources: monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json (SEC-6.2 rewrite note, three conflicting prediction values)

stronger_arguments.description: Relative-station-magnitude test (E-PRED-B) is the dome's one plausibly discriminating prediction for the eclipse and should be separately Pass-tracked. The dome claims Hartland (coverage 0.80, W004 -26.2 nT) will show a LARGER signal than Ebro (coverage 0.95, W004 -21.7 nT). A naive Chapman-only model (signal ~ coverage) predicts Ebro > Hartland. If August 2026 measurements show HAD > EBR, that is plausibly dome-favorable discrimination. Our 8b.3 treats the Pass band (magnitude-only) as the discriminator and argues it is not; we should promote E-PRED-B to the Tier 3 discussion as the test that plausibly DOES discriminate, concede prospective force, and note that even a HAD > EBR result would require comparison against a Chapman+latitude-weighted Sq-dynamo model before being called dome-only discriminating.

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-191 correctly implements the hole #12 recommendation from SEC-8b.c1 — the three-part edit plus both optional follow-ons are all present. The section now does apply our non-discrimination thesis to PRED-095's Pass band, instead of silently strengthening the dome's framing. The ADOPTED (FULL as steel-man) label mirrors 8b.2's existing pattern, maintaining the fair-audit posture. Surfacing the non-discrimination critique in the TLDR is a genuine improvement for skim readers. The (d) Unchanged extension from one item to two accurately reports the two structural critiques that survive adoption. The Fail-clause tension paragraph — while phrasing-inconsistent (see minor hole above) — surfaces a real logical tension the prior version ignored. As a patch implementing ISS-1190, this is well-executed.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Implementation correctness of hole #12 is not a full defense of 8b.3. The remaining major holes — dual-baseline structure, unaddressed E-PRED-A/B/C/D model-discriminating tests, Kim & Chang attribution overreach — are either inherited from the prior version or emerge from engaging with the dome's full prediction corpus rather than just the cited Pass band. These require follow-on expansions (not part of EXP-191 scope) to fully close. The verdict on SEC-8b.3 holds (ADOPTED as steel-man), but the section can still be strengthened.

deeper_analysis: The EXP-191 patch successfully converts a Pass-band-friendly presentation into a non-discrimination-applying one, closing the major hole that prompted ISS-1190. Reading the new 8b.3 from a dome-defender's perspective, the strongest counter-attacks shift in predictable ways: (1) Attack the attribution: 'We never said we calibrated on Kim & Chang 2018; your specific attribution is wrong.' Our text could hedge to the same body of eclipse-magnetic observations. (2) Point to E-PRED-A through E-PRED-D: 'We published four model-discriminating tests; your non-discrimination argument does not address them.' Our text should cross-link to Part 4.2/Part 9 treatment. (3) Highlight the BOU baseline: 'Our dual-baseline structure means if observations come in at -10 nT, the BOU band passed; your -17 to -21 focus is narrow.' Our text should acknowledge dual-baseline. (4) Contest 'cannot be triggered': 'A -20 nT signal with timing or morphology inconsistent with Chapman would trigger the Fail-clause; your cannot-be-triggered is too strong.' Our text (per anticipated_objections) intended to soften to in-tension-with, but the harder phrasing survived. None of these attacks overturn the verdict — the non-discrimination thesis is correct in its core claim — but they each reveal places where 8b.3 could be firmed up. Additionally, the three-conflicting-predictions critique (from WIN-025 fingerprint) is a free evidence upgrade we have not yet deployed in 8b.3 and would strengthen the adversarial case without new research. Other side of the argument — where a dome defender is partly right: the dome IS making a genuinely prospective commitment by publishing a numerical Pass band before the event, and E-PRED-B (HAD > EBR relative magnitudes) is at least facially a test that could come out dome-favorable and globe-unfavorable. We do not retract the prospectivity concession; we narrow what a Pass would buy the model.

citations_verified: Kim & Chang (2018) Advances in Space Research 61:2040-2054 — verified as source for WIN-068 39-eclipse ensemble in data/wins.json detail_evidence
Chapman (1933) Terrestrial Magnetism and Atmospheric Electricity 38:3 — verified in WIN-068 detail_evidence; this is the mechanism paper
WIN-068 code_analysis.derives_from_dome:false — verified in data/wins.json (current value)
PRED-095 registration 2026-04-04 in our tracker, window closes 2026-08-12 — verified in data/predictions.json
Section 8b.2 uses ADOPTED (FULL as steel-man) label — verified in data/sections.json part8b.html p8b-2-geometry-path
Dome dual-baseline publication (BOU 2017 -10.9 nT | W004 empirical -22.24 nT) — verified in raw-text/03-predictions.txt lines 19-24
citations_failed: Van Camp et al. (2001) superconducting-gravimeter null-result — inherited from prior 8b.3, not re-verified in this review; flagged for future citation audit

Kim & Chang 2018 as calibration source for the dome's -17 to -21 nT Pass band — NOT verified; dome materials cite baselines (BOU 2017, W004 empirical) and V12 H(r)/r FSF formula without mentioning Kim & Chang 2018. This is the source of the moderate attribution hole above.
citations_unchecked: Yamazaki & Maute (2021) Sq-dynamo framework — referenced in WIN-068 detail but not directly cited in 8b.3
Wang et al. (1997) Mohe gravity-eclipse paper — inherited L2 citation

notes_on_scope: This review covers only 8b.3 (the EXP-191 target). The other 11 open holes from SEC-8b.c1 (4.2 collision, missing CSS, citation-compliance audit, claim_key/resolution-criteria unshipped, 8b.2 Row 2 overclaim, 8b.3 L1 calibration framing, hero phrase, 0.4% Nullarbor source, chg-006 omission, PRED-095/PRED-TIER-1 disambiguation, conditional escape clauses) remain open per EXP-191 completion_notes. Those should be tracked under separate issues/expansions; this review does not address them.
